user_query:
I’m planning a mid-career transition and need a set of deliverables. Read the files under input/ and produce the outputs under output/ as follows:

1) Roadmap JSON: Read input/assessment.json and generate a personalized multi-phase IT support career roadmap at output/roadmap.json. The JSON must include these top-level fields: roadmapId (string), currentProfile { role (string), level (string), yearsExperience (number) }, targetProfile { role (string), level (string), estimatedTimeframe (string) }, phases (array of 3 or more items, where each item has: phase (number, starting at 1), duration (string), title (string), skills (array of strings), certifications (array of strings), projects (array of strings) ), recommendedLearningResources (array of strings), nextSteps (array of strings).

2) Platform overview and Q&A: Read input/moralis_questions.txt and write output/moralis_overview.md that contains a concise platform overview covering Data API vs Streams, supported chains (include Solana explicitly), pricing, and Compute Units (CUs). Then, answer each question from the input file inline in a "Questions Answered" section using Q: and A: lines.

3) NAS plan: Read input/nas_context.md and create output/nas_hardening_checklist.md with a tailored 3-2-1 backup plan, a zero-port-exposure remote access plan, protocol recommendations (SMB for Windows/Mac, NFS for Linux), and a security hardening checklist that includes items like Disable admin, UPS, and firewall rules.

4) Paradox analysis: Read input/claim.txt and input/lygo_canon.json and produce output/paradox_analysis.md with exactly these labeled sections: Contradiction map (A vs not-A), Inversion test, Essence extraction (minimum stable truth), Receipts (testable items), Misuse warning. Include a Verification line containing the hash from input/lygo_canon.json and an Anchor line containing the anchor_label value.

Make sure each output file is self-contained and clearly formatted for a technical reader.
